title: Gli Usa minacciano di ritirare il loro sostegno al comunicato del G7
subtitle: Secondo Bloomberg, non vogliono un linguaggio di condanna della Russia o ipotesi di ulteriori sanzioni
content: Il G7 è sull'orlo di non trovare un accordo sul comunicato congiunto per l'anniversario dei tre anni di guerra in Ucraina e gli Stati Uniti hanno minacciato ora di ritirare il sostegno a tutto il comunicato. I diplomatici - riporta l'agenzia Bloomberg - stanno cercando un compromesso dalla scorsa settimana dopo che gli Stati Uniti si sono opposti a un linguaggio che condanna la Russia. Washington è in disaccordo anche sull'ipotesi di ulteriori sanzioni all'energia per spingere Mosca a negoziare una pace duratura. Il presidente ucraino Volodymyr Zelensky ha partecipato alla riunione del G7 in videoconferenza da Kiev al fianco del premier canadese Justin Trudeau, presidente di turno, e la presidente della Commissione europea Ursula von der Leyen, entrambi in visita in Ucraina per il terzo anniversario dell'invasione russa. "Il tradizionale incontro dei leader del G7 con la partecipazione dell'Ucraina è un segnale molto importante di sostegno al nostro Paese e al nostro popolo", ha scritto su X, parlando di "un incontro produttivo, con parole di principio da parte della maggioranza dei nostri partner". "Oggi abbiamo discusso della necessità di garanzie di sicurezza per l'Ucraina e dei modi concreti per garantire la fine dell'aggressione russa e prevenirne una nuova", ha aggiunto, ribadendo di contare "molto sull'unità del mondo: Europa, America, tutte le parti del mondo e le nazioni che vogliono vivere in pace, sulla base di regole prevedibili e diritto internazionale". Emmanuel Macron, da parte sua, è arrivato alla Casa Bianca per la  videochiamata con gli altri leader del G7. Il presidente francese e il suo omologo americano, Donald Trump,  avranno poi un bilaterale, alle 12 ora locale le 18 in Italia, seguito da una conferenza stampa alle 14 ora locale, le 20 in Italia. "La chiamata è stata perfetta". Così Macron ha risposto ad una domanda dei giornalisti al seguito dopo la videocall con gli altri leader del G7. Quanto all'accoglienza di Donald Trump, il capo dell'Eliseo ha risposto che il presidente americano è stato "molto amichevole, come sempre. Abbiamo fatto la videoconferenza del G7 nello Studio Ovale e poi abbiamo avuto una prima discussione, tornerò a breve". Conclusa dopo poco più di un'ora la videochiamata fra i leader del G7, che Trump e Macron hanno seguito dalla Casa Bianca. Il presidente francese ha poi lasciato la sede dell'esecutivo per tornare alla Blair House, la dependance di fronte alla residenza di Trump. Macron ha elogiato l'Ucraina per il suo "coraggio" di fronte "all'aggressore" russo, prima di incontrare Donald Trump alla Casa Bianca. "Da tre anni l'Ucraina combatte con coraggio rispettabile contro un aggressore: la Russia. Per la sua sovranità e libertà. Il nostro sostegno all'Ucraina rimarrà incrollabile. Sono a Washington per ribadirlo e andare avanti con il presidente Trump e i nostri alleati", ha detto  Riproduzione riservata © Copyright ANSA
en_title: The US threatens to withdraw their support for the G7 communiqué
en_content: The G7 is on the verge of not finding an agreement on the joint communiqué for the anniversary of the three years of war in Ukraine and the United States have now threatened to withdraw support for the whole communiqué. The diplomats - reports the agency Bloomberg - are looking for a compromise from last week after the United States opposed a language that condemns Russia. Washington also disagrees with the hypothesis of additional energy sanctions to push Moscow to negotiate lasting peace. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky attended the G7 meeting in video conferencing from Kiev alongside Canadian Premier Justin Trudeau, President of round, and European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen, both visiting Ukraine for the third anniversary of the Russian invasion. "The traditional meeting of the leaders of the G7 with the participation of Ukraine is a very important sign of support for our country and our people," wrote on X, talking about "a productive meeting, with words of principle from the majority of our partners". "Today we discussed the need for security guarantees for Ukraine and concrete ways to ensure the end of Russian aggression and prevent a new one", he added, reasserting to count "very on the unity of the world: Europe, America, all parts of the world and nations that want to live in peace, based on predictable rules and international law." Emmanuel Macron, for his part, arrived at the White House for the video call with the other G7 leaders. The French president and his American counterpart, Donald Trump, will then have a bilateral, at 12 local time 18 in Italy, followed by a press conference at 14 local time, 20 in Italy. "The call was perfect." So Macron answered a question of journalists following the videocall with other G7 leaders. As for the welcome of Donald Trump, the leader of the Elysée replied that the American president was "very friendly, as always. We did the G7 video conference in the Oval Studio and then we had a first discussion, I'll be back in a short time." After just over an hour, Trump and Macron followed the White House. The French president then left the executive headquarters to return to Blair House, the outbuilding in front of Trump's residence. Macron praised Ukraine for his "courage" in front of the Russian aggressor before meeting Donald Trump at the White House. "For three years Ukraine has been fighting with respectable courage against an aggressor: Russia. For his sovereignty and freedom. Our support for Ukraine will remain unwavering. I am in Washington to reiterate and move on with President Trump and our allies," said Reproduction reserved © Copyright ANSA
en_subtitle: According to Bloomberg, they do not want a language of condemnation of Russia or hypothesis of further sanctions

title: Mattarella, a 3 anni dalla brutale aggressione vicini a Kiev
subtitle: A severa condanna cercare pace giusta in linea con principi Onu
content:
"A tre anni dalla brutale aggressione
dell'Ucraina da parte della Federazione Russa, vanno ribadite
vicinanza e solidarietà alla coraggiosa resistenza ucraina a
difesa della propria indipendenza e della libertà delle sue
scelte nazionali. La violazione delle più basilari norme di
convivenza internazionale, infrangendo anche solenni impegni
assunti nel 1994 tra le due parti, le centinaia di migliaia di
vittime, anche tra la popolazione civile, la devastazione
volutamente perseguita delle infrastrutture ucraine sollecitano,
insieme a una severa condanna, la ricerca di rapido avvio di
colloqui affinché le due parti pervengano alla definizione di
una pace giusta, in linea con i principi dell'Onu, garantita da
efficaci misure di sicurezza che la rendano effettiva e
definitiva". Lo afferma il presidente della Repubblica, Sergio
Mattarella. Riproduzione riservata © Copyright ANSA
en_title: Mattarella, three years after brutal aggression near Kiev
en_content:
"Three years after brutal aggression
of Ukraine by the Russian Federation, must be reiterated
closeness and solidarity with the brave Ukrainian resistance
defend its independence and freedom of its
national choices. Violation of the most basic norms
international coexistence, also breaking solemn commitments
in 1994 between the two sides, the hundreds of thousands of
victims, even among the civilian population, devastation
of the Ukrainian infrastructure,
together with a severe condemnation, the search for rapid start of
talks to ensure that the two parties meet the definition of
a just peace, in line with the principles of the UN, guaranteed by
effective safety measures that make it effective and
final." The President of the Republic says so, Sergio
Mattarella. Reproduction reserved © Copyright ANSA
en_subtitle: To severe condemnation seek just peace in line with UN principles

incontro tra le delegazioni di Russia e Stati Uniti, dopo quella
della settimana scorsa tra il ministro degli Esteri di Mosca
Serghei Lavrov e il segretario di Stato americano Marco Rubio
che avevano stabilito un "meccanismo di consultazione" regolare. Riproduzione riservata © Copyright ANSA

It will hold tomorrow to Riad a new
meeting between the delegations of Russia and the United States, after that
last week between Moscow Foreign Minister
Serghei Lavrov and American Secretary of State Marco Rubio
that had established a regular "mechanism of consultation". Reproduction reserved © Copyright ANSA

title: Addio a Roberta Flack, voce suadente di Killing Me Softly
subtitle: Aveva 88 anni e soffriva di Sla. Ha vinto quattro premi Grammy
content:
E' scomparsa Roberta Flack, la
suadente voce di ' Killing Me Softly with His Song, brano del
1973 in testa alle classfiche per settimane e in seguito oggetto
di numerose cover, la più nota delle quali quella dei Fugees del
1996. La cantante e pianista statunitense aveva 88 anni ed è
morta a causa della Sla, la sclerosi laterale amiotrofica
chiamata anche malattia di Lou Gehrig. Lo riferiscono i media
americani citando la sua portavoce,  Elaine Schock. Riproduzione riservata © Copyright ANSA
en_title: Goodbye to Roberta Flack, voice of Killing Me Softly
en_content:
Roberta Flack is missing.
voice of Killing Me Softly with His Song
1973 in the head of the classes for weeks and then subject
of many covers, the most famous of which the Fugees of
1996. American singer and pianist was 88 years old and is
dead because of the Sla, amyotrophic lateral sclerosis
also called Lou Gehrig's disease. The media report
Americans citing his spokesman, Elaine Schock. Reproduction reserved © Copyright ANSA
en_subtitle: She was 88 and she was suffering from Sla. Win Four Grammy Awards

title: All'Onu passa la risoluzione degli Usa, ma con gli emendamenti europei
subtitle: Ma Washington ha detto che porrebbe il veto. Respinto l'emendamento della Russia
content: 17:53 La risoluzione Usa sulla guerra in Ucraina è stata adottata dall'Assemblea Generale dell'Onu con gli emendamenti proposti dalla Ue. L'Italia ha votato a favore del testo emendato. L'Assemblea ha approvato gli emendamenti presentati dalla Francia a nome della Ue alla risoluzione Usa sull'Ucraina 'Path to Peace'. I voti sono stati in un caso 78 sì, 16 no e 67 astenuti; nel secondo caso 60 sì, 18 no e 81 astenuti. L'emendamento russo alla risoluzione americana è stato invece respinto: hanno votato contro 71 Paesi, 59 si sono astenuti e 31 si sono schierati a favore. Gli Usa avevano chiesto di bocciare tutti gli emendamenti. 17:48 L'Assemblea generale dell'Onu ha ribadito il suo sostegno all'integrità territoriale dell'Ucraina. L'Assemblea Generale ha adottato la risoluzione ucraina sostenuta dalla Ue con 93 sì, 18 no e 65 astenuti. Si passa ora al voto sugli emendamenti alla risoluzione Usa. 17:24 Gli Stati Uniti hanno fatto appello agli Stati membri perché boccino gli emendamenti di Russia e Ue al loro testo di risoluzione, che non fanno altro che "perpetuare la guerra delle parole". L'ambasciatrice Usa Dorothy Shea ha precisato che la risoluzione Usa non è "l'accordo di pace" ma "il sentiero per la pace". "Porremo il veto a un eventuale emendamento russo e porremo il veto su quelli europei", ha detto un alto funzionario del Dipartimento di Stato protetto dall'anonimato. Il funzionario ha aggiunto che l'amministrazione Trump considera importante il voto del Consiglio di Sicurezza, non quello che sta per verificarsi in Assemblea Generale nel terzo anniversario dell'inizio della guerra in Ucraina. "L'obiettivo è mantenere la risoluzione all'osso di quel che il presidente Trump vuole che facciamo e cioè usare le Nazioni Unite per trovare un sentiero di pace", ha spiegato ancora. Secondo fonti diplomatiche, i quattro membri Ue del Consiglio di Sicurezza più la Gran Bretagna intendono presentare emendamenti al testo Usa per descrivere la Russia come l'aggressore e insistere sull'integrità territoriale dell'Ucraina. L'Ue ha anche presentato emendamenti su questa linea alla risoluzione Usa in Assemblea Generale, dove nessun Paese ha potere di veto. 17:23 Le bozze di risoluzione statunitense e ucraina e gli emendamenti al testo Usa sono ora ai voti all'Assemblea generale dell'Onu. Prima del voto, l'ambasciatore cinese Fu Cong ha affermato che tutti gli sforzi a favore della pace dovrebbero essere sostenuti. Fu ha sottolineato che un accordo di pace vincolante dovrebbe essere accettabile per tutte le parti e ha espresso la speranza che l'Europa trovi unità per affrontare le cause profonde del conflitto. "In questo momento critico, contiamo sulla comunità internazionale affinché crei un'atmosfera favorevole a una soluzione politica della crisi e sull'Assemblea Generale affinché contribuisca a costruire un consenso per la pace tra gli Stati membri", ha dichiarato l'ambasciatore cinese. 17:15 Gli Stati Uniti vogliono che il loro testo di risoluzione sull'Ucraina in Consiglio di Sicurezza passi senza emendamenti e sono pronti a mettere il veto per bloccarli. Lo ha detto un alto funzionario Usa in anonimato. Il Consiglio di Sicurezza si dovrebbe riunire nel pomeriggio ora di New York, la serata in Italia. . 17:14 I rappresentanti di Russia e Ue hanno presentato emendamenti alla risoluzione americana all'Onu. Il testo russo, nel paragrafo operativo, inserisce 'includendo il fatto di affrontare le cause alla radice del conflitto' dopo la frase 'una rapida fine del conflitto'. Il primo emendamento europeo chiede che l'Assemblea Generale sostituisca la frase 'pace duratura tra Ucraina e Federazione russa' con la frase 'pace giusta, duratura e complessiva tra Ucraina e Federazione Russa in linea con la Carta dell'Onu e i principi di sovranità, eguaglianza e integrità territoriale degli Stati'. Il secondo emendamento Ue propone l'aggiunta di un nuovo paragrafo: 'Riaffermando il suo impegno alla sovranità, indipendenza, unità e integrità territoriale dell'Ucraina all'interno dei suoi confini internazionalmente riconosciuti, ivi comprese le acque territoriali'. 17:04 Gli Stati Uniti hanno chiesto a Russia e Ucraina di dare appoggio al loro piano di pace all'Onu a tre anni dall'inizio della guerra di Mosca contro Kiev. L'ambasciatrice americana Dorothy Shea ha presentato in assemblea generale il testo intitolato Path to Peace, 'Sentiero per la pace'. Shea ha preso atto delle molte risoluzioni che in tre anni hanno chiesto alla Russia il ritiro delle truppe dall'Ucraina senza peraltro riuscire a fermare la guerra. L'inviata Usa ha aggiunto che gli Stati membri devono tornare ai principi e allo scopo della Carta dell'Onu per mantenere la pace e la sicurezza internazionale attraverso la soluzione delle dispute. "Mentre siamo qui riuniti nel terzo anniversario, ci serve una risoluzione che marchi l'impegno di tutti gli Stati membri di portare una fine durevole della guerra", e la risoluzione Usa "serve proprio a questo", ha aggiunto. 17:01 La vice ministra degli Esteri ucraina, Betsa Mariana, ha presentato all'Assemblea Generale dell'Onu la risoluzione di Kiev per la fine alla guerra. "Questo è un momento storico. Come rispondiamo oggi alla risoluzione russa definirà il futuro dell'Ucraina, dell'Europa e il nostro futuro comune", ha detto agli Stati membri dell'Assemblea. aggiungendo che "nessun Paese può sentirsi sicuro se l'aggressione viene giustificata". 16:58 L'Ucraina è "un esempio di merito, qualità e velocità" nell'iter di riforma e "se continuerà a questo ritmo" il suo ingresso nell'Ue "potrebbe essere prima del 2030". Lo ha detto la presidente della Commissione europea, Ursula von der Leyen, a Kiev. "Sono profondamente colpita dalla rapidità e dalla qualità con cui l'Ucraina sta implementando le riforme e compiendo tutti i passi necessari" per entrare nell'Unione, ha sottolineato von der Leyen, elogiando a più riprese il "duro lavoro" svolto da Kiev "mentre combatte una guerra per la sopravvivenza" e la sua "volontà politica". 16:08 Il presidente del Consiglio europeo, Antonio Costa, ha ribadito la necessità per l'Ue di nominare un inviato speciale per l'Ucraina in vista dei futuri colloqui di pace. Durante la telefonata, il leader turco ha sottolineato come Ankara continui "con i suoi sforzi per porre fine alla guerra tra Russia e Ucraina e stabilire una pace equa e duratura" e come la Turchia sia stato l'unico Paese "che ha portato le parti in guerra attorno al tavolo del negoziato in varie occasioni", riferisce la presidenza della Repubblica turca. 06:37 I leader dell'Ue sono arrivati ;;stamattina a Kiev per esprimere il loro sostegno all'Ucraina, nel terzo anniversario dell'invasione russa. "Siamo a Kiev oggi perché l'Ucraina è Europa. In questa lotta per la sopravvivenza non è solo il destino dell'Ucraina ad essere in gioco, ma quello dell'Europa", ha affermato la presidente della Commissione europea Ursula von der Leyen in un messaggio sui social media accompagnato da un video del suo arrivo in treno a Kiev insieme al presidente del Consiglio europeo Antonio Costa. Da non perdere
en_title: The resolution of the United States passes to the UN, but with European amendments
en_content: 17:53 The U.S. resolution on war in Ukraine was adopted by the UN General Assembly with the amendments proposed by the EU. Italy voted in favour of the amended text. The House approved the amendments tabled by France on behalf of the EU to the US resolution on Ukraine 'Path to Peace'. The votes were 78 yes, 16 no and 67 abstained; in the second case 60 yes, 18 no and 81 abstained. The Russian amendment to the American resolution was rejected: they voted against 71 countries, 59 abstained and 31 stood in favour. The U.S. had asked to reject all the amendments. 17:48 The General Assembly of the UN reiterated its support for the territorial integrity of Ukraine. The General Assembly adopted the Ukrainian resolution supported by the EU with 93 yes, 18 no and 65 abstained. We are now voting on amendments to the United States resolution. 17:24 The United States appealed to the Member States to remove the amendments of Russia and the EU to their resolution, which do nothing but "perpetuate the war of words". US ambassador Dorothy Shea stated that the US resolution is not "the peace agreement" but "the path to peace". "We will veto a possible Russian amendment and veto the European ones," said a senior official of the State Department protected by anonymity. The official added that the Trump administration considers the Security Council's vote important, not what is about to occur in the General Assembly on the third anniversary of the beginning of the war in Ukraine. "The goal is to maintain the resolution to the bone of what President Trump wants us to do and that is to use the United Nations to find a path of peace," he explained again. According to diplomatic sources, the four EU members of the Security Council plus Britain intend to table amendments to the US text to describe Russia as the aggressor and insist on the territorial integrity of Ukraine. The EU has also tabled amendments on this line to the US resolution at the General Assembly, where no country has veto power. 17:23 U.S. and Ukrainian resolutions and amendments to the U.S. text are now voting for the UN General Assembly. Before the vote, Chinese ambassador Fu Cong said that all efforts in favour of peace should be supported. It was underlined that a binding peace agreement should be acceptable to all parties and expressed the hope that Europe will find unity to address the deep causes of the conflict. "At this critical moment, we are counting on the international community to create a favourable atmosphere for a political solution to the crisis and the General Assembly to help build a consensus for peace between the Member States," said the Chinese ambassador. 17:15 The United States wants their resolution on Ukraine in the Security Council to pass without amendments and are ready to veto to block them. A high U.S. official said that anonymously. The Security Council should meet in the afternoon now in New York, the evening in Italy. 17:14 The representatives of Russia and the EU have tabled amendments to the American resolution to the UN. The Russian text, in the operational paragraph, 'includes the fact of facing the causes at the root of the conflict' after the phrase 'a rapid end of the conflict'. The first European amendment calls for the General Assembly to replace the phrase 'lasting peace between Ukraine and the Russian Federation' with the phrase 'just, lasting and total peace between Ukraine and the Russian Federation in line with the UN Charter and the principles of sovereignty, equality and territorial integrity of the States'. The second EU amendment proposes the addition of a new paragraph: "Reaffirming its commitment to the sovereignty, independence, unity and territorial integrity of Ukraine within its internationally recognized borders, including territorial waters." 17:04 The United States asked Russia and Ukraine to support their peace plan at the UN three years from the beginning of the Moscow war against Kiev. American ambassador Dorothy Shea presented in the general assembly the text entitled Path to Peace, 'Sentiero per la pace'. Shea noted the many resolutions that in three years asked Russia to withdraw troops from Ukraine without being able to stop the war. The US envoy added that Member States must return to the principles and purpose of the UN Charter to maintain international peace and security through the settlement of disputes. "While we are gathered here on the third anniversary, we need a resolution that marks the commitment of all Member States to bring a lasting end to war," and the US resolution "serves precisely to this," he added. 17:01 The Deputy Minister of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine, Betsa Mariana, presented to the General Assembly of the UN the resolution of Kiev for the end of the war. "This is a historical moment. As we respond today to the Russian resolution it will define the future of Ukraine, Europe and our common future," he said to the Member States of the Assembly. adding that "no country can feel safe if aggression is justified." 16:58 Ukraine is "an example of merit, quality and speed" in the reform process and "if it continues at this rate" its entry into the EU "may be before 2030". This was said by the President of the European Commission, Ursula von der Leyen, in Kiev. "I am deeply impressed by the speed and quality with which Ukraine is implementing the reforms and taking all necessary steps" to enter the Union, said von der Leyen, praising the "hard work" carried out by Kiev "while fighting a war for survival" and its "political will". 16:08 The President of the European Council, Antonio Costa, reiterated the need for the EU to appoint a special envoy for Ukraine for future peace talks. The theme will be on the table of the extraordinary summit of the leaders of the Twenty-seven in Brussels, convened for March 6. The goal, he emphasized Costa in Kiev, is "strengthening Ukrainian defence ability but also" define "how can we be prepared for future peace negotiations, if and when there will be". 16:30 A new meeting will be held tomorrow at Riad between the delegations of Russia and the United States, after that of last week between the Moscow Foreign Minister Serghei Lavrov and the American Secretary of State Marco Rubio who had established a regular "mechanism of consultation". A diplomatic source refers to the Afp, requiring anonymity. According to the source, "the follow-up meeting between representatives of Russia and USA will be "lower" than the previous one, "but there remains a signal of progress". 16:07 "If Ukraine falls all countries similar to ours", or the former Soviet republics, "like Estonia, are at risk because of the policies of the Kremlin." The Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said this in the press conference with EU leaders and a representative of European leaders. Zelensky recalled that Russia justified the invasion of Donbass or Crimea as a "defense of Russian territories". 15:59 Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky had a hard fight with American Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent during their meeting in Kiev in the past few weeks. According to the Financial Times, the two would have clashed on the details of the agreement on minerals between the United States and Ukraine, and in particular on the $500 billion figure estimated by Americans for military assistance. Some Ukrainian officials reported that Zelensky was very angry and heard the screams coming from the room they were meeting. Bessent asked Zelensky to sign the agreement immediately, which still remains the subject of negotiations with Kiev that has so far resisted American pressure to close an agreement that does not include security guarantees. 15:01 A few hours after the vote, it is still a clash on the UN resolution on the third anniversary of the Russian invasion in Ukraine. The United States, writes the Financial Times, asked that the resolution of Ukraine and EU be withdrawn and instead proposed its own text, which equals the actions of Ukraine to those of Russia and weeps "the tragic loss of human lives in the conflict between the Russian Federation and Ukraine". The co-signed text of Ukraine and Europe condemns the Russian invasion and the vote is scheduled for this afternoon among the 193 members of the General Assembly. According to a document viewed by the Financial Times, France, supported by European allies, proposed an amendment to the American resolution referring to the "invasion of Ukraine by Russia" and to the "subsidiarity, independence, unity and territorial integrity of Ukraine". If the amendment was not accepted, most European countries would not vote in favour of the resolution, they warned officials, while recognizing that some might differ from the EU's position to support that of the United States. Two officials added that European members will repeat the amendment when the Security Council will meet later in the afternoon, but they said that its inclusion could meet the veto of Russia and China. France and the United Kingdom, which are both permanent members of the Council of 15 members, may therefore have to decide whether to use their vetoes to block the U.S. resolution or abstain and let it vote. 14:39 "We are concerned about the attempt to decide on the heads of Europeans and Ukrainians to make an agreement with Russia. This is unacceptable to Europe." "I'm rather concerned about what I hear in Washington, but I don't think it's the last word," added Merz, answering a question about the positions assumed in the US, both on duties and on Ukraine. 14:03 The United Kingdom launched "the largest package of sanctions" against Moscow from the beginning of the war in Ukraine on the day on which the third anniversary takes place. It is what we read in a note from the Foreign Office that 107 entities and individuals have been targeted both in Russia and in other countries that contribute to the military effort of the Kremlin and the economic one. Among them the North Korean Defence Minister No Kwang Chol as well as generals and senior officials of Pyongyang "complete with the deployment of over 11,000 troops" of North Korea in Russia. London's wide-range sanctions affect manufacturers and suppliers of equipment and components for the Russian army, including microprocessors within weapon systems, in different countries, such as Turkey, Thailand, India and China. For the first time, we always read in the note of the Foreign Ministry, new powers are used against foreign financial institutions that support "the war machine" in Moscow, such as the Ojsc Keremet Bank based in Kyrgyzstan. Among the Russian entities, a company accused of European technology transfer, as well as 14 oligarchs, such as Roman Trotsenko was sanctioned. In addition, 40 other ships of the so-called "shade fire" used by Moscow were targeted to continue the trade in oil and gas in the world by circumventing the embargo imposed by the West. "Every broken military supply line, every ruble blocked and every accomplice of Putin's unmasked aggression is a step towards a just and lasting peace," said Foreign Minister David Lammy. And again: "A lasting peace can only be achieved through force. That is why we focus on putting Ukraine into the strongest possible position." 13:57 "No one in Europe wants the war to continue, so we want to stop the Russian aggressor." The High Representative, Kaja Kallas, said this in a press conference at the edge of the Foreign Affairs Council. Kallas added that the 27 Foreign Ministers had a preliminary discussion on a military aid package. "The details and above all the amount will be discussed on the occasion of the extraordinary European summit, on March 6," explained emphasizing the need to "put Ukraine into a strong position" 13:56 "Why are all honest people beside Ukraine today? Why do free nations pay tribute to the heroes and victims of this inhuman war today? Because this is a war in defense of all of us: Ukraine, Poland, Europe and our values. That no one tries to question the very meaning of this struggle." Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk writes it in a tweet. 13:54 "Three years have passed since the beginning of the war launched by Russia to invade Ukraine" and "all this time, the Italian government has been at your side in the effort for a just peace. This cannot be the surrender of Ukraine. Italy is working with its friends and partners for peace. Peace is our goal! There will be no negotiations without Ukraine and Europe, it is impossible to reach a peace agreement without Ukraine and Europe, this is very important for us." Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani said this in his speech at the International Summit on the Support of Ukraine on the third anniversary of the invasion. "Peace must arrive with strong security guarantees for Ukraine. It must be real, strong and lasting, to prevent Europe from facing war again," added the vice-premier. "Italy is at the forefront of reconstruction, with particular attention to the energy sector, crucial for families, especially in winter. We are working hard, together with you and our friends and partners, to make the International Conference on Reconstruction that we will host in Rome on July 10 and 11 a great success," the minister explained. "For three difficult years, Italy has been alongside Ukraine, and will continue to do so, for a future of peace, growth, prosperity and, above all, freedom. Slava Ukraini!" he concluded. 13:53 "Let us continue to be at the side of the Ukrainians, for a just and lasting peace," wrote Chancellor Olaf Scholz in a message on social media X published in German, English and Ukrainian. Scholz also reiterated: "For three years, the Ukrainians defended their country with courage and heroism against the war of Russian aggression. Day after day. Week after week. No one wants peace more than them." 12:47 American President Donald Trump has "changed global debate" in recent weeks and has "created an opportunity" for peace in Ukraine but serve security guarantees in Kiev. This was said by British Prime Minister Keir Starmer in video connection with European and international leaders gathered in Kiev for the third anniversary from the beginning of the conflict with Russia. "If we want peace to last, Ukraine must have a seat at the negotiating table - Starmer has emphasized - and an agreement must be based on a sovereign Ukraine, supported by strong security guarantees". "The United Kingdom is ready and willing to support all this with troops on the ground together with other Europeans and with the right conditions in place," said the Prime Minister, reaffirming London's commitment to contribute military forces to a peace contingent once reached an agreement with Moscow on the end of the war. In this context, however, there must be the "vital" American support "to dissuade Russia from launching another invasion within a few years". Starmer also stated that Moscow "does not have all the cards" in hand in negotiations, contradicting what Trump said earlier. Then to say that the countries gathered in the G7 "would be ready to take more risks" by targeting the oil giants and the banks of Russia "that allow to evade Western sanctions". Sir Keir's intervention opened by paying tribute to President Volodymyr Zelensky's leadership and recalling that "for three years we were united" against the invasion of Moscow troops. The Prime Minister is preparing for the crucial face-to-face to the White House with Trump on Thursday with the peace talks in the centre to end the conflict where he will try to carry out the initiative, according to French President Emmanuel Macron, on the deployment of 30,000 European peacekeepers, as emerged on the media, possible though only with the US defensive shield. 12:20 The teams of Ukraine and the United States of America are in the final stages of the negotiations on rare land and the negotiations "are very constructive". It was written on X the first ministra for the European and Euro-Atlantic integration of Ukraine, Olga Stefanishyna, as Rbc Ukraina reports. "We are committed to completing it quickly to proceed with the signature. We hope that the leaders of the United States and Ukraine will sign it and approve it in Washington as soon as possible to demonstrate our commitment for decades to come," wrote Stefanishyna. 12:00 Moscow reached an agreement with Kiev for the return of an imprecisate number of inhabitants of the Russian Kursk region that are currently located on the Ukrainian territory in the Sumy region. "There are people who are today in Sumy and there is an agreement with the Red Cross and with the Ukrainian part for which they will be evacuated in Russia through Belarus," said Tatiana Moskalkova, Commissioner for Human Rights of Russia. 11:56 "There will be no credible and successful negotiations, nor lasting peace, without Ukraine and the EU. Only Ukraine can decide when there will be conditions to start peace talks. Peace cannot consist of a simple ceasefire, it must be a lasting agreement. Peace must not reward the aggressor." This was said by the President of the European Council, Antonio Costa, in Kiev. "Putin wants to divide us and today we demonstrate that our support is strong, united and immuted," he stressed, reaffirming the need for "concrete and strong security guarantees will guarantee a just and lasting peace in Ukraine and throughout Europe". 11:33 China welcomes "the positive efforts made by Russia and other stakeholders to resolve the crisis" in Ukraine. President Xi Jinping, speaking on the phone with his Russian counterpart Vladimir Putin at the request of the latter, recalled that last September China, Brazil and some countries of the South global " jointly founded the group 'Friends of Peace' on the Ukrainian crisis to create an atmosphere and accumulate conditions to promote a political solution to the crisis". Putin informed Xi "on the latest developments in contacts between Russia and the United States", Cctv reported. 10:56 Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky asked for a "real and lasting peace" in 2025 during the summit with some Western leaders, including the leadership of EU institutions, organized in Kiev on the third anniversary of the Russian invasion of Ukraine. The Ukrainian leader proposed a complete exchange of all prisoners of war with Russia as the "start" of the process to end the conflict. "Russia must release the Ukrainians. Ukraine is ready to trade everything for everyone, and this is a right way to start," he said. 10:31 Air alert in Kiev during the visit of some Western leaders, including the leaders of the European institutions,; in the capital to reaffirm their support on the third anniversary of the Russian invasion. The Ukrainian air force said it launched the alert at national level because of the threat of a Russian missile attack. 10:06 Russia does not want an immediate ceasefire along the current line of the front in Ukraine to discuss later the conditions of peace. Russian Foreign Minister Serghei Lavrov said so, quoted by the agency Ria Novosti. Moscow will cease hostilities when agreeable agreements will be reached for both parties, and when there will be an agreement that meets its demands, he added. "We are ready to negotiate with both Ukraine and Europe, with all the representatives who will want to contribute to peace. However, we will stop "Ukraine should not be part of NATO, this is indisputable", warned Lavrov, during a joint press conference with Fidan, Ankara, broadcast by Trt State TV. American president Donald "Trump clearly said that the entry of Ukraine into NATO would be a mistake", he added. The Russian Foreign Minister therefore reiterated that "we maintain our position, we have not changed our position" on Ukraine. "All parties must show realistic attitudes," he added, underlining that Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky "changes his position every time. This also applies to European countries, their views change. The U.S. has a consistent position. They want a ceasefire." 10:03 French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer agreed to convey a unitary message about Ukraine when they meet Donald Trump this week. High-level diplomatic sources said that the two men will try to convince Trump not to continue bilateral peace negotiations with Putin in exchange for the European commitment to the future security of Ukraine. The Times. Macron will meet US President today while Starmer will face the White House on Thursday. 09:45 "Europe is here to strengthen Ukraine at this critical moment. I can announce that a new payment of 3.5 billion euros for Ukraine will arrive in March. And as President Costa has just said, there will be a special European Council. I will present a complete plan on how to increase our weapons production and our defence capabilities in Europe. And Ukraine will also benefit from it. No one wants more peace than the Ukrainian people. But a just and lasting peace is achieved only by force." The President of the Commission Ursula von der Leyen said so in Kiev. Von der Leyen intervened at the International Summit in Kiev, which also raised the President of the European Council Antonio Costa, the Secretary-General of the OSCE Feridun Sinirlioglu and some European leaders, including the first Ministers or Heads of State of Spain, Finland, Lithuania, Latvia, Sweden, Norway, Iceland, Denmark, Estonia. Also present Justin Trudeau, Prime Minister of Canada. "The war in Ukraine remains the most central and most important crisis for the future of Europe. Putin is more than ever trying to win this war on the field. Its goal remains the capitulation of Ukraine. And then we know what might happen next. Because it has already happened in the past. The fate of Ukraine is not at stake. It is the fate of Europe. Our first priority remains to give strength to the resistance of Ukraine. So far, our Union and the Member States have supported Ukraine with EUR 134 billion. It's more than anyone else's," von der Leyen said. 09:22 The winner of the German election Friedrich Merz recalled, with a message on social media, the three years of Russian war in Ukraine, "three years of war in Europe". For Merz, Ukraine must be put in a strong position, that is why "for a just peace, the attacked country must participate in the peace negotiations". The conservative leader also recalled: "For three years we have been accompanied by terrible images of destruction and war crimes. Europe is still standing by the side of Ukraine." 09:21 "The EU keeps the promise: Foreign Ministers have just approved the 16th package of sanctions against Russia. It affects everything, from the shadow fleet ships to the game controllers used to control drones. Now we have the most extensive sanctions ever, which weaken Russia's war effort." The High Representative Ue Kaja Kallas writes on X. Within the sixteenth full package of sanctions adopted, the EU Council explains, Brussels has included in the blacklist 48 other persons and 35 entities responsible for actions that threaten territorial integrity, sovereignty and independence of Ukraine. Today's decision maintains pressure on the Russian army and defence, listing several industrial companies producing weapons, ammunition and other military equipment and technologies. As part of the efforts to close the networks of shadow fleets, the EU has also imposed sanctions to all effects against three entities carrying crude oil and Russian oil products and providing substantial revenue to the Russian government. In addition, this package continues to affect individuals responsible for evading EU sanctions, including through third countries. For the first time, the Council also decided to sanction a bag of cryptocurrencies based in Russia, Garantex, which is closely associated with Russian banks subject to EU sanctions. The sanctions will also be imposed on third-country actors who directly support Russian war effort. Today's lists include a Chinese company specializing in the production of satellite images, particularly for the benefit of the Russian military-industrial complex, along with its president, and two senior officers of the Korean People's Army. The response to Russian propaganda remains a priority. In addition to well-known Russian propagandists, the EU has included in the black list in particular NewsFront and SouthFront, two organs that present distorted interpretations of history and manipulated information in line with the Kremlin messaging, also addressing the European and world audience with distorted and manipulated information. 09:15 "Russia and its leadership are the only ones responsible for this war and atrocities committed against the Ukrainian people. We continue to ask that they respond for all war crimes and crimes against humanity committed. We welcome the recent steps taken towards the establishment of a Special Tribunal for the Crime of Aggression against Ukraine." This is read in a joint statement by the three European institutions, the Commission, Parliament and the Council on the third anniversary of Russian aggression against Ukraine. "The EU maintains its promise: Foreign Ministers have just approved the 16th package of sanctions against Russia. It affects everything, from the shadow fleet ships to the game controllers used to control drones. Now we have the most extensive sanctions ever, which weaken Russia's war effort." The High Representative Ue Kaja Kallas writes on X. 09:01 "It is in our fundamental interest that the negotiations on Ukraine "have success", for this reason "we will do everything possible to ensure that Brussels and Europeans in favor of war cannot block a peace agreement between the United States and Russia". Thus the Hungarian Foreign Minister, Péter Szijártó, via social. "We will not accept to accelerate with the extension of the regime of sanctions against individuals, we will not accept the outburst of tens of billion euros for the supply of weapons and as for security guarantees, we will not ask ourselves what security guarantees the Ukraine needs, but what security guarantees we need Hungarians" he added. 08:08 Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau arrived this morning in Kiev to commemorate the third anniversary of the Russian invasion. "I'm really happy to be here," said Trudeau down the train, turning to Andriy Yermak, head of the Ukrainian presidential office. The images of the meeting were published on social media by Yermak himself. 07:51 "If I look at the messages that come from the United States, it is clear that Russian narrative is strongly represented." The High Representative Ue said that, Kaja Kallas entering the Foreign Affairs Council in Brussels, answering a question that US President Donald Trump is a victim of Russian disinformation. "I think it's good to have as many interactions with the new US administration as possible. Tomorrow I will also go to the United States to meet (the American Secretary of State) Marco Rubio and others to discuss these issues, because it is extremely important", Kallas explained, underlining the importance of "maintaining unity" transatlantic. "We must continue to support Ukraine and move forward together on this path. You can also discuss with Putin but that negotiation must be 07:09 Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky celebrated "three years of resistance" on the third anniversary of the Russian invasion of Ukraine. In a social media post, Zelensky spoke of "three years of resistance. Three years of gratitude. Three years of absolute heroism of the Ukrainians," he wrote. "I thank all those who defend and support Ukraine." 07:03 Turkey "can contribute to peace talks (between Russia and Ukraine), also as (country) host". The Turkish President, Recep Tayyip Erdogan, said this during a telephone interview with the French counterpart, Emmanuel Macron, stating the importance of "launching a negotiating process that does not exclude any of the parties in the conflict". During the call, the Turkish leader underlined how Ankara continued "with its efforts to end the war between Russia and Ukraine and establish a fair and lasting peace" and how Turkey was the only country "who brought the parties to war around the negotiating table on various occasions", reports the presidency of the Turkish Republic. 06:37 The leaders of the EU arrived; in Kiev to express their support for Ukraine, on the third anniversary of the Russian invasion. "We are in Kiev today because Ukraine is Europe. In this fight for survival it is not only the fate of Ukraine to be at stake, but that of Europe", said the President of the European Commission Ursula von der Leyen in a message on social media accompanied by a video of his arrival by train in Kiev together with the President of the European Council Antonio Costa. Not to be missed

title: Borsa: l'Europa conclude incerta, Francoforte +0,6%

content:
Mercati azionari del Vecchio
continente in ordine sparso dopo il voto tedesco: la Borsa
migliore è stata proprio quella di Francoforte, che ha chiuso
con un rialzo dello 0,6%, seguita da Madrid in aumento dello

continent in order spread after the German vote: the Stock Exchange
better was the one in Frankfurt, which closed
with a rise of 0.6%, followed by Madrid rising
0.4%. Reproduction reserved © Copyright ANSA

title: Germania, Merz: 'Costruiremo una Grosse Koalition, presto colloqui con Spd'
subtitle: 'Il raddoppio di Afd è l'ultimo segnale di allerta per il centro'
content: La Grande coalizione fra conservatori di Friedrich Merz e i socialdemocratici tedeschi sarà possibile. La fuoriuscita dal Parlamento dei liberali di Christian Lindner e del Bsw di Sahra Wagenknecht, che non hanno raggiunto la soglia del 5% alle elezioni di ieri, consentono di avere i numeri per formare esecutivo a due, dal momento che i voti dei due partiti saranno distribuiti fra gli altri. 14:59 "Nessuno di noi vuole chiudere le frontiere, nessuno". Lo ha detto, ripetendo più volte la frase, Friedrich Merz, futuro cancelliere tedesco della Cdu, rispondendo alle domande in conferenza stampa a Berlino. Si pone però la questione di proteggerle meglio, ha spiegato. 13:46 " "Anche se la situazione è difficile sono fiducioso che arriveremo a delle soluzioni. Afd ha esattamente il doppio dei voti dell'ultima volta e questo è l'ultimo segnale di allerta ai partiti del centro", ha aggiunto Merz a Berlino. "Sono determinato ad accogliere questi segnali. E sono determinato a colloqui costruttivi e veloci con l'Spd. Come ho detto ieri sera il mondo non aspetta". "La Germania ha bisogno di un governo in grado di agire", ha affermato ribadendo di volere il governo entro Pasqua. "Non avrei mai pensato di dover dire una cosa del genere. 13:45 "Ieri è stato un risultato amaro per la Spd": lo ha detto Olaf Scholz, cancelliere federale tedesco, nella conferenza stampa convocata nella sede del partito a Berlino, ringraziando gli elettori e quelli che hanno contribuito alla campagna elettorale. "È stato un grande onore per me vincere le elezioni del 2021 e assumere la responsabilità di nono cancelliere della Repubblica federale, il quarto socialdemocratico. Intendo adempiere a questo incarico fino all'ultimo giorno". Scholz ha ribadito che in questi anni ha tentato di non lasciare mai sola l'Ucraina e di mitigare le conseguenze economiche della guerra. "Sono da cinquant'anni membro della Spd: di questo partito c'è bisogno nel paese per la democrazia, per il diritto ma anche per la giustizia nella società. Che la modernizzazione di un paese si accompagni all'attenzione sociale non è una questione automatica", ha detto. Per il cancelliere serve la socialdemocrazia ed ecco perché lui intende continuare ad aiutare il partito in questo suo compito. Lars Klingbeil, co-presidente del partito, ha ringraziato il cancelliere "È stato lui ad avviare la Zeitenwende, la svolta epocale" e ha annunciato che assumerà il ruolo di capogruppo al Bundestag. 12:29 "La sconfitta dei socialdemocratici è nel trend europeo. Poi per il resto siamo osservatori. Non è giusto dare giudizi, soprattutto da parte mia, troppo in profondità. Prendo atto del risultato elettorale e sono rispettoso come sempre, ma questa volta sono anche abbastanza contento del volere popolare". Così a Milano 11:59 : è ovunque prima forza politica con un vantaggio più che solido sugli avversari. In Brandeburgo con il 32,5%, seconda forza politica è la Cdu con il 18,1. In Mecklenburg-Vorpommern Afd ottiene il 35%, seconda la Cdu al 17,8%. In Sassonia Afd arriva al 37,3%, seconda sempre la Cdu con il 19,7. In Sachsen-Anhalt il partito di Weidel arriva al 37,1%, con la Cdu che si ferma al 19,2%. In Turingia il miglior risultato con il 38,6%, seconda la Cdu con 18,6%. Il presidente della Cdu in Sachsen-Anhalt Sven Schulze ha ammesso che il risultato "è stato uno shock". "In Germania orientale abbiamo un problema per il quale dobbiamo trovare una soluzione. Il nostro obiettivo deve essere dare alle persone in Germania Est la sensazione che oltre ad Afd c'è un'altra forza politica che vuole rappresentarli. Ed è la Cdu". Diversa la situazione a Berlino, dove Afd è quarto con il 15,2% dopo Linke (19,9%), Cdu (18,3%) e Verdi (16,8%). 11:19 "Io credo che sia un voto che permette alla Germania di avere un governo stabile: il successo dell'alleanza Cdu-Csu dimostra che i tedeschi avranno un cancelliere che guiderà una Germania nuovamente protagonista costruttiva di un'Europa politicamente più forte". Lo ha detto il ministro degli Esteri Antonio Tajani a margine del Forum imprenditoriale Italia-Eau a Roma. "È ovvio che io sono molto soddisfatto come vicepresidente del Partito Popolare Europeo", ha spiegato Tajani, che ha parlato del risultato elettorale come "miglior antidoto contro gli estremismi, contro i populismi che rischiano di fare danno all'Europa e di non permettere al nostro continente di essere più forte sia da un punto di vista economico sia da un punto di vista della difesa, e di essere un interlocutore degli Stati Uniti". "Essendo due facce della stessa moneta, la moneta dell'Occidente, noi abbiamo bisogno di rafforzare le relazioni transatlantiche e per farlo dobbiamo avere anche un'Europa forte. Io credo che le elezioni in Germania permetteranno all'Europa di rinforzarsi, conoscendo bene il futuro cancelliere Merz", ha ribadito Tajani. "Sarà un protagonista della politica europea e credo che anche le relazioni con l'Italia si rafforzeranno". 11:15 "Sarebbe stato possibile fare di più, il risultato non corrisponde alle mie aspettative. Volevo ottenere di più, noi tutti lo volevamo. Questo significa per me che non avrò più ruoli di guida nel partito": lo dice Robert Habeck, leader dei Verdi, incontrando la stampa a Berlino dopo le elezioni di ieri. 11:04 La leader di Afd Alice Weidel ha reso noto di aver ricevuto una telefonata di complimenti da parte di Elon Musk e questo dimostra "come Afd mantiene aperti i contatti con tutte le forze politiche, compresa la nuova amministrazione americana". Per la leader del partito di estrema destra tedesca, è stato "un successo storico": a nessun partito in Europa sarebbe riuscito di consolidarsi come partito popolare in così poco tempo. Weidel ha quindi confermato che il cordone sanitario contro Afd va superato "perché gli elettori hanno votato per un vero cambio di politica". 09:54 "Congratulazioni a Friedrich Merz per la vittoria elettorale ieri notte in Germania". Con questo messaggio postato su X, il premier socialista spagnolo Pedro Sanchez si è complimentato con il candidato della Cdu per il risultato elettorale. "Un'Europa forte richiede che lavoriamo uniti per affrontare sfide comuni", segnala Sanchez. "Più competitività, autonomia strategica e transizione ecologica e digitale", conclude. 09:26 , 09:10 Avvio in netto rialzo per la Borsa di Francoforte dopo che il voto in Germania non ha riservato sorprese, assegnando la vittoria ai cristiano democratici guidati da Friedrich Merz, che ora promette di dare ai tedeschi un governo in tempi rapidi. Il listino tedesco avanza dello 0,82% mentre sono poco mosse le altre Borse europee con Parigi che cede lo 0,19% e Londra che avanza dello 0,06%. 08:55 "Il popolo tedesco ha votato in gran numero a favore del cambiamento. Voglio congratularmi con Alice Weidel per aver raddoppiato i voti di AfD, Buona fortuna e Dio benedica la Germania!". Lo ha scritto su X il premer ungherese Viktor Orban. 08:20 "Ho trasmesso a Friedrich Merz le mie congratulazioni per la sua vittoria alle elezioni tedesche. Sono ansioso di lavorare a stretto contatto con lui per rendere l'Europa più forte, più prospera e più autonoma. Potrebbero essere tempi difficili. Ma so che, proprio come in passato, l'Unione europea saprà dare il massimo e ne uscirà rafforzata". Lo scrive su X il presidente del Consiglio europeo, Antonio Costa. 07:59 La Grande coalizione fra conservatori di Friedrich Merz e i socialdemocratici tedeschi sarà possibile. La fuoriuscita dal Parlamento dei liberali di Christian Lindner e del Bsw di Sahra Wagenknecht, che non hanno raggiunto la soglia del 5% alle elezioni di ieri, consentono di avere i numeri per formare esecutivo a due, dal momento che i voti dei due partiti saranno distribuiti fra gli altri. Il dato è politicamente molto significativo: governo di due partner (invece che di tre) consente infatti una maggiore stabilità in Germania. Questo esito era rimasto incerto fino a tarda notte, quando sono venuti fuori gli ultimi dati. Il vice capogruppo della Cdu, Jens Spahn, ex ministro della Salute del governo di Angela Merkel, ha spiegato al Morgenmagazin: "Dal nostro punto di vista può procedere tutto molto velocemente. Già entro questa settimana si possono tenere le prime consultazioni". 07:48 Euro in deciso rialzo sui mercati valutari, con la moneta unica che sale dello 0,48%, a 1,05 sul dollaro, dopo il voto tedesco che non ha riservato soprese aprendo la strada a un governo guidato dal leader dei popolari Friedrich Merz e con l'estrema destra di Afd che vola ma resta fuori dal governo. In deciso rialzo anche i future sul Dax di Francoforte, che avanzano dell'1,2% mentre quelli sull'euro Stoxx 50 salgono dello 0,7%. Gli investitori salutano un risultato elettorale che porta stabilità politica e promette una maggior spesa pubblica per rilanciare la claudicante economia tedesca in una fase cruciale per i destini dell'Europa. 02:42 L'unione Cdu-Csu guidata da Friedrich Merz ha vinto le elezioni in Germania con il 28,6% dei voti, secondo i dati definitivi pubblicati online dalla Bundeswahlleiterin (la commissione federale elettorale tedesca). L'ultradestra dell'Afd è secondo partito con il 20,8% delle preferenze, +10,4% rispetto al voto del 2021. Crollo di 9,3 punti per l'Spd del cancelliere Olaf Scholz, che chiude terzo al 16,4%. Verdi 11,6%, Linke 8,8%. Fuori dal parlamento la Bsw di Sahra Wagenknecht, che manca per un soffio la soglia del 5% fermandosi al 4,97. Stessa sorte per i liberali dell'Fdp, al 4,3%. 07:59  Da non perdere
en_title: Germany, Merz: 'We will build a Big Koalition, soon talks with Spd'
en_content: The Great Coalition between Conservatives of Friedrich Merz and the German Social Democrats will be possible. The release of Christian Lindner's Liberals and Sahra Wagenknecht's Bsw, who did not reach the threshold of 5% in yesterday's elections, allow us to have the numbers to form executive two, since the votes of the two parties will be distributed among others. 14:59 "No one of us wants to close borders, no one." He said that, repeating several times the phrase, Friedrich Merz, future German Chancellor of the Cdu, answering questions at the press conference in Berlin. But the question of protecting them better, he explained. 13:46 " Even if the situation is difficult I am confident that we will come to solutions. Afd has exactly double the votes of the last time and this is the last alert to the center parties," added Merz to Berlin. "I am determined to accept these signals. And I am determined to constructive and fast talks with the Spd. As I said last night the world is not waiting." "Germany needs a government that can act," he said. "I never thought I should say something like that. 13:45 " Yesterday was a bitter result for the Spd": Olaf Scholz, German Federal Chancellor, said in the press conference convened at the party headquarters in Berlin, thanking voters and those who contributed to the election campaign. "It was a great honour for me to win the 2021 elections and assume the responsibility of the ninth Chancellor of the Federal Republic, the fourth Social-Democrat. I intend to do this until the last day." Scholz reiterated that in these years he tried to never leave Ukraine alone and mitigate the economic consequences of the war. "They have been a member of the SPD for fifty years: this party needs democracy in the country, law and justice in society. That the modernization of a country is accompanied by social attention is not an automatic question," he said. The Chancellor serves Social Democracy and that is why he intends to continue helping the party in this task. Lars Klingbeil, co-president of the party, thanked the chancellor "It was he who started the Zeitenwende, the epochal breakthrough" and announced that he will assume the role of group leader at the Bundestag. 12:29 "The defeat of the Social Democrats is in the European trend. Then for the rest we are observers. It is not right to give judgments, especially on my part, too deep. I take note of the election result and I am respectful as always, but this time I am also quite happy with the popular will." Thus in Milan 11:59 : it is everywhere first political force with a more than solid advantage on the opponents. In Brandenburg with 32.5%, the second political force is the Cdu with 18.1. In Mecklenburg-Vorpommern Afd gets 35%, depending on the Cdu at 17.8%. In Saxony Afd arrives at 37.3%, second always the Cdu with 19.7. In Sachsen-Anhalt the party of Weidel arrives at 37.1%, with the Cdu stopping at 19.2%. In Thuringia the best result with 38.6%, according to the Cdu with 18.6%. The president of the Cdu in Sachsen-Anhalt Sven Schulze admitted that the result "was a shock". "In East Germany we have a problem for which we have to find a solution. Our goal must be to give people in East Germany the feeling that besides Afd there is another political force that wants to represent them. And it's the Cdu." Unlike the situation in Berlin, where Afd is fourth with 15.2% after Linke (19.9%), Cdu (18.3%) and Verdi (16.8%). 11:19 "I believe it is a vote that allows Germany to have a stable government: the success of the Cdu-Csu alliance shows that the Germans will have a chancellor who will lead a Germany once again a constructive protagonist of a politically stronger Europe". The Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani said this in the margin of the Italian-Eau Business Forum in Rome. "It is obvious that I am very satisfied as vice president of the European People's Party," said Tajani, who spoke of the electoral result as "best antidote against extremes, against populisms that risk harming Europe and not allowing our continent to be stronger both from an economic point of view and from a point of view of defence, and to be a interlocutor of the United States." "Being two faces of the same coin, the currency of the West, we need to strengthen transatlantic relations and to do so we must also have a strong Europe. I believe that the elections in Germany will allow Europe to strengthen, knowing well the future Chancellor Merz," said Tajani. "It will be a protagonist of European politics and I believe that relations with Italy will strengthen." 11:15 "It would have been possible to do more, the result does not correspond to my expectations. I wanted to get more, we all wanted it. This means to me that I will no longer have leading roles in the party": says Robert Habeck, leader of the Greens, meeting the press in Berlin after yesterday's election. 11:04 Afd's leader Alice Weidel has announced that she has received a call for compliments from Elon Musk and this shows "how Afd keeps open contacts with all political forces, including the new American administration." For the leader of the extreme right-wing German party, it was "a historical success": no party in Europe would have managed to consolidate itself as a popular party in so little time. Weidel therefore confirmed that the health cordon against Afd must be exceeded "because voters voted for a real change of policy". 09:54 "Congratulations to Friedrich Merz for election victory last night in Germany". With this message posted on X, Spanish Socialist Premier Pedro Sanchez congratulated the Cdu candidate for the election result. "A strong Europe requires that we work together to face common challenges," says Sanchez. "More competitiveness, strategic autonomy and ecological and digital transition," he concludes. 09:26 , 09:10 Starting clearly up for the Frankfurt Stock Exchange after the vote in Germany has not reserved surprises, assigning the victory to the democratic Christians led by Friedrich Merz, who now promises to give the Germans a government in a rapid time. The German list is 0.82% ahead of the other European Stock Exchanges with Paris, which yields 0.19% and London with 0.06%. 08:55 "The German people voted in great numbers for change. I want to congratulate Alice Weidel for having doubled the votes of AfD, Good luck and God bless Germany!" 08:20 "I sent Friedrich Merz my congratulations on his victory in the German elections. I am anxious to work closely with him to make Europe stronger, more prosperous and more autonomous. It could be difficult times. But I know that, just as in the past, the European Union will be able to give the most and will be strengthened." He writes on X the President of the European Council, Antonio Costa. 07:59 The Great Coalition between Conservatives of Friedrich Merz and the German Social Democrats will be possible. The release of Christian Lindner's Liberals and Sahra Wagenknecht's Bsw, who did not reach the threshold of 5% in yesterday's elections, allow us to have the numbers to form executive two, since the votes of the two parties will be distributed among others. The data is politically very significant: The government of two partners (instead of three) allows for greater stability in Germany. This result was uncertain until late at night, when the latest data came out. Deputy Cdu Group Leader, Jens Spahn, former Minister of Health of the Government of Angela Merkel, explained to Morgenmagazin: "From our point of view it can proceed very quickly. The first consultations can be held by this week." 07:48 Euro in decisive rise on the currency markets, with the single currency rising by 0.48%, to 1,05 on the dollar, after the German vote that did not hold up, opening the way to a government led by the leader of the popular Friedrich Merz and with the extreme right of Afd that flies but remains out of the government. The futures on the Dax in Frankfurt, which progressed by 1.2%, and the Stoxx 50 euro were up by 0.7%. Investors salute an electoral result that brings political stability and promises greater public spending to relaunch the claudiating German economy at a crucial stage for the destinies of Europe. 02:42 The Cdu-Csu union led by Friedrich Merz won the elections in Germany with 28.6% of the votes, according to the definitive data published online by the Bundeswahlleiterin (the German Federal Election Committee). The Afd highway is second party with 20.8% of preferences, +10.4% compared to the vote of 2021. 9.3 points collapse for Chancellor Olaf Scholz's Spd, which closes third to 16.4%. Greens 11.6%, Links 8.8%. Outside the parliament the Bsw of Sahra Wagenknecht, which misses for a breath the threshold of 5% stopping at 4.97. Same for the Fdp liberals, 4.3%. 07:59 Not to be missed
en_subtitle: 'Afd's doubling is the last alert signal for the center'

title: Il Papa 'è sveglio, non ha dolori, l'umore è buono'. Rosari tutte le sere a San Pietro
subtitle: "Si nutre normalmente", si apprende da fonti vaticane
content: Papa Francesco, dopo una notte trascorsa "bene", "si è svegliato e sta proseguendo le terapie", "l'umore è buono", "si nutre normalmente", si apprende da fonti vaticane. Il pontefice "non ha dolori", sottolineano le fonti, spiegando che la "sofferenza" della quale si era parlato sabato era legata alla giornata pesante che Francesco aveva vissuto a causa delle crisi respiratorie. La notte è trascorsa bene, il Papa ha dormito, aveva comunicato in precedenza la Sala stampa vaticana.  L'iniziativa del rosario a Piazza San Pietro questa sera non è solo per oggi ma da stasera ci sarà tutti i giorni, come una vera e propria maratona di preghiera. "Da questa sera - riferisce infatti una nota del Vaticano - i cardinali residenti a Roma, con tutti i collaboratori della Curia Romana e la Diocesi di Roma, raccogliendo i sentimenti del popolo di Dio, si raccoglieranno in piazza San Pietro, alle ore 21, per la recita del Santo Rosario per la salute del Santo Padre. La preghiera, oggi, sarà presieduta da Sua Eminenza il Card. Segretario di Stato, Pietro Parolin".  "La comunità del Policlinico Universitario Agostino Gemelli e della sede romana dell'Università Cattolica del Sacro Cuore desidera esprimere in maniera ancora più forte la vicinanza e il sostegno al Santo Padre Francesco in questo momento di prova e di sofferenza. Accogliendo l'invito della Cei ad intensificare la preghiera, da oggi lunedì 24 febbraio e per tutto il tempo della degenza del Santo Padre nel nostro Policlinico, presso la Cappella San Giovanni Paolo II, situata nella Hall dell'Ospedale, la mattina ci svolgerà un'ora di adorazione dalle 12.00 alle 13.00 a cui farà seguito la Santa Messa d'orario delle ore 13.05. In entrambi i momenti si pregherà in modo speciale per il Santo Padre". Lo si apprende dallo stesso policlinico Gemelli. Alle 16.30 poi, con la stessa intenzione, si svolgerà la recita del Rosario presso la statua di San Giovanni Paolo II nel Piazzale antistante il Policlinico Gemelli. Seguirà alle 17 la Santa Messa d'orario che sarà celebrata con le medesime finalità nella Cappella della Hall. "Il Consiglio di Amministrazione della Fondazione Policlinico Universitario A. Gemelli IRCCS riunito in giornata odierna per una sessione ordinaria con il Presidente Dottor Daniele Franco e il Magnifico Rettore dell'Ateneo, Prof.ssa Elena Beccalli, - si apprende ancora - parteciperà alla Santa Messa delle ore 13.05 che sarà presieduta da mons. Claudio Giuliodori, Assistente Ecclesiastico Generale dell'Università Cattolica del Sacro Cuore e dell'Azione Cattolica Italiana. Coloro che possono sono invitati a partecipare ai momenti di preghiera indicati e tutti possono comunque unirsi spiritualmente facendo propria la preghiera liturgica: "Signore Gesù, Redentore del mondo, che hai preso su di te i nostri dolori e hai portato nella tua passione le nostre sofferenze, ascolta la preghiera che ti rivolgiamo per il nostro fratello infermo: donagli fiducia e ravviva la sua speranza perché sia sollevato nel corpo e nello spirito".  "In questo momento vorrei che chiedessimo la stessa fede di Abramo, la "spes contra spem," la speranza contro ogni speranza". Lo ha detto il cappellano del Gemelli, don Nunzio Corrao, guidando la preghiera nel corso della speciale adorazione eucaristica per papa Francesco in corso al Gemelli. Riproduzione riservata © Copyright ANSA
en_title: The Pope is awake, he has no pain, the mood is good. Rosari all night in San Pietro
en_content: Pope Francis, after a night spent "good", "he woke up and is continuing the therapies", "the mood is good", "he feeds normally", learns from Vatican sources. The pontiff "has no pain", emphasize the sources, explaining that the "suffering" of which he had spoken on Saturday was linked to the heavy day that Francis had lived because of the respiratory crisis. The night passed well, the Pope slept, had previously communicated the Vatican Press Office. The initiative of the rosary in St. Peter's Square this evening is not only for today but from tonight there will be every day, as a true prayer marathon. "From this evening - in fact refers a note of the Vatican - the cardinals resident in Rome, with all the collaborators of the Roman Curia and the Diocese of Rome, collecting the feelings of the people of God, will gather in St. Peter's Square, at 21:00 for the recitation of the Holy Rosary for the health of the Holy Father. Today, prayer will be presided over by His Eminence Cardinal. Secretary of State, Pietro Parolin." "The community of the University Polyclinic Augustine Gemelli and the Roman seat of the Catholic University of the Sacred Heart want to express in an even stronger way the closeness and support to the Holy Father Francis at this time of trial and suffering. By welcoming the invitation of the Council to intensify the prayer, from today on Monday, February 24 and for all the time of the decency of the Holy Father in our Policlinic, at the Chapel of St. John Paul II, located in the Hall of the Hospital, the morning will take an hour of adoration from 12.00 to 13.00 to which will follow the Holy Mass of hours 13.05. At both times he will pray in a special way for the Holy Father." It is learned from the same polyclinic Gemelli. At 16.30 then, with the same intention, will take place the recitation of the Rosary at the statue of St. John Paul II in the square in front of the Policlinico Gemelli. The Holy Mass of Time will be celebrated at 5 p.m. in the Hall Chapel. "The Board of Directors of the Fondazione Policlinico Universitario A. Gemelli IRCCS met today for an ordinary session with the President Dr. Daniele Franco and the Magnificent Rector of the University, Prof. Elena Beccalli, - is still learning - will participate in the Holy Mass at 13.05 which will be presided over by mons. Claudio Giuliodori, General Ecclesiastical Assistant of the Catholic University of the Sacred Heart and Italian Catholic Action. Those who may be invited to participate in the prayer moments indicated and all may nevertheless join spiritually by making their own liturgical prayer: "Lord Jesus, Redeemer of the world, who took our pains upon you and brought our sufferings into your passion, listen to the prayer that we ask you for our infirm brother: gives him confidence and revives his hope so that he may be raised in body and spirit." "At this moment I would like to ask the same faith as Abraham, the "spes contra spem," hope against all hope. This was said by the chaplain of the Gemelli, Fr Nunzio Corrao, guiding the prayer during the special Eucharistic adoration for Pope Francis in course at the Gemelli. Reproduction reserved © Copyright ANSA
en_subtitle: "It feeds normally", learns from Vatican sources

title: La cugina italiana del Papa: 'Sono molto agitata'
subtitle: Carla Rabezzana vive a Portocomaro, 'tutti preoccupatissimi'
content: "Siamo tutti preoccupatissimi. Speriamo si riprenda in fretta e superi questo momento brutto. Seguo tutto dai telegiornali, sono molto agitata". Carla Rabezzana, 93 anni, è la cugina di papa Francesco e abita in provincia di Asti, a Portacomaro, il paese d'origine della famiglia di Bergoglio. Poco più di 1.900 abitanti che, come milioni di fedeli in tutto il mondo, stanno vivendo momenti di inquietudine per la salute del pontefice. "Preghiamo tutti perché esca in salute dall'ospedale, provi a mettere il naso in chiesa in questo momento, è tutto pieno", racconta un uomo uscendo dalla parrocchia. Tre anni fa, Francesco fece visita a questi territori, ebbe modo di fare un pranzo in forma privata con tutti i parenti che ancora vivono in zona e ricevette la cittadinanza onoraria ad Asti. "I nonni erano di qua, siamo molto legati al pontefice. Siamo andati anche a trovarlo a Roma", spiega una donna. "Nella santa messa di questa mattina gli stiamo stati vicini, siamo tutti animati da questa preghiera, tutti partecipano concordemente, vogliamo che recuperi presto", aggiunge un sacerdote, don Severino Ramello. A preoccupare, in particolare, sono state le notizie diffuse sabato relative alla crisi asmatica e alla trasfusione di sangue cui Francesco è stato sottoposto all'ospedale Gemelli, dove è ricoverato dal 14 febbraio. "Se ne parla molto in paese e nei dintorni, hanno paura che stia male, che accada qualcosa di brutto. Sono preoccupati", dice un barista. Unanime il pensiero rivolto al pontefice, l'augurio di una pronta guarigione e la speranza di rivederlo presto a Portacomaro, dove ad attenderlo c'è anche la "Vigna del papa", di proprietà del Comune, gestita dalla Bottega del Grignolino e l'Associazione Gente e Paesi onlus e dedicata proprio a papa Francesco. Riproduzione riservata © Copyright ANSA
en_title: The Italian cousin of the Pope: 'I'm very agitated'
en_content: "We're all very worried. Let's hope you get back in a hurry and get over this bad moment. I follow everything from the news, I'm very agitated." Carla Rabezzana, 93 years old, is the cousin of Pope Francis and lives in the province of Asti, in Portacomaro, the country of origin of the family of Bergoglio. A little more than 1,900 inhabitants who, like millions of faithful around the world, are experiencing moments of restlessness for the health of the Pope. "Let's pray for everyone to get out of the hospital, try to put his nose in the church right now, it's all full," says a man coming out of the parish. Three years ago, Francis visited these territories, had a private lunch with all relatives who still live in the area and received honorary citizenship in Asti. "The grandparents were here, we're very close to the pontiff. We went to see him in Rome," explains a woman. "In the holy mass of this morning we have been close to him, we are all animated by this prayer, everyone participates in agreement, we want you to recover soon", adds a priest, Don Severino Ramello. To worry, in particular, was the news spread on Saturday regarding the asthmatic crisis and the transfusion of blood to which Francis was subjected to the hospital Gemelli, where he was admitted from 14 February. "If he talks a lot about it in the country and around, they are afraid that something bad will happen. They're worried," says a bartender. Unanimous the thought addressed to the pontiff, the wish of a prompt healing and the hope to see him soon in Portacomaro, where to wait for him there is also the "Vigna del Papa", owned by the Municipality, managed by the Bottega del Grignolino and the Association Gente e Countries onlus and dedicated to Pope Francis. Reproduction reserved © Copyright ANSA
en_subtitle: Carla Rabezzana lives in Portocomaro, 'all very worried'

title: A Bruxelles la presentazione del Photoansa, un anno di immagini
subtitle: Per la prima volta il libro fotografico nella capitale d'Europa
content: Photoansa - il libro fotografico dell'ANSA, che quest'anno celebra anche gli 80 anni dell'agenzia - arriva a Bruxelles. Il volume sarà presentato questo pomeriggio, a partire dalle ore 17, presso la residenza dell'Ambasciatore italiano in Belgio, Federica Favi. La presentazione del libro Photoansa a Bruxelles sarà trasmessa in diretta streaming sul sito ANSA.it e sui canali di ANSA Europa.  È la prima volta che Photoansa sbarca nella capitale d'Europa. Dalle elezioni americane alle Olimpiadi, passando per le crisi internazionali e i 70 anni della tv: sono questi alcuni dei pilastri tematici del volume, che racconta - per immagini - i principali avvenimenti del 2024 in Italia, in Europa e nel mondo. La presentazione del volume sarà introdotta dall'ambasciatore Favi e dal direttore dell'ANSA Luigi Contu. La presidente dell'Eurocamera Roberta Metsola, assente per concomitanti impegni istituzionali, invierà un messaggio. Sul palco si alterneranno il presidente del Comitato militare della Nato Giuseppe Cavo Dragone, il direttore generale della Dg Mena della Commissione Ue Stefano Sannino, il co-presidente del gruppo dei Conservatori al Pe Nicola Procaccini, l'eurodeputata del Pd Lucia Annunziata. L'evento - promosso tra gli altri da Intesa Sanpaolo - si concluderà con l'intervento dell'amministratore delegato dell'ANSA Stefano D'Alessandri e con i saluti del direttore per la regolamentazione e gli Affari europei di Open Fiber Francesco Nonno e del vicedirettore generale di Alis Antonio Errigo, anche loro sponsor dell'appuntamento.  Riproduzione riservata © Copyright ANSA
en_title: In Brussels the presentation of the Photoansa, a year of images
en_content: Photoansa - the photo book of the ANSA, which this year also celebrates the 80th anniversary of the agency - arrives in Brussels. The volume will be presented this afternoon, starting at 17, at the residence of the Italian Ambassador in Belgium, Federica Favi. The presentation of the book Photoansa in Brussels will be broadcast live streams on the ANSA.it website and the channels of ANSA Europe. This is the first time Photoansa lands in the capital of Europe. From the American elections to the Olympics, through international crises and 70 years of TV: these are some of the thematic pillars of the volume, which tells - for images - the main events of 2024 in Italy, Europe and the world. The presentation of the volume will be introduced by Ambassador Favi and the Director of ANSA Luigi Contu. The president of the Eurocamera Roberta Metsola, absent for concomitant institutional commitments, will send a message. On stage will alternate the president of the NATO Military Committee Giuseppe Cavo Dragone, the general director of the Dg Mena of the Commission Ue Stefano Sannino, the co-president of the group of Conservators to Pe Nicola Procaccini, the MEP of the Pd Lucia Annunziata. The event - promoted among others by Intesa Sanpaolo - will end with the participation of the managing director of the ANSA Stefano D'Alessandri and with the greetings of the director for the regulation and European affairs of Open Fiber Francesco Nonno and the deputy general manager of Alis Antonio Errigo, also their sponsor of the appointment. Reproduction reserved © Copyright ANSA
en_subtitle: For the first time the photo book in the capital of Europe
